deptName: ช่างอากาศยาน
standardRef: EASA PART 66 MODULE 5 DIGITAL TECHNIQUES/ELECTRONIC INSTRUMENT SYSTEMS
learningOutcomes:
Understand and be able to explain knowledge about the Electronic Instrument
Systems and general arrangement of typical electronic/digital aircraft systems with Built in test
equipment, demonstrate the System operation of Glass cockpit correctly, identify checking
the Electronic Instrument System. with safety awareness.
objectives:
1. To provide knowledge about the Electronic Instrument Systems and general
arrangement of typical electronic/digital aircraft systems with Built in test equipment.
2. To provide the Safety awareness in checking the Electronic Instrument System tasks.
3. To provide attitudes, and Habit desirable while performing the Electronic Instrument
System tasks.
4. To provide the application concepts while perform checking the glass cockpit
devices, with safety awareness.
competencies:
1. Describe and codify knowledge concerned the Fundamental of each particular
Instrument Glass cockpit correctly.
2. Identify checking the Electronic Instrument System with the safety mildness.
3. Perform testing the System operation of typical electronic/digital aircraft systems correctly.
4. Demonstrate the System operation of Glass cockpit correctly.
description:
To study and practice in the concept of Electronic Instrument Systems, Typical
Electronic/Digital Aircraft Systems for example of ACARS-ARINC Communication and Addressing
and Reporting System, EFIS-Electronic Flight Instrument Systems ECAM-Electronic Centralized
Aircraft Monitoring, FBW-Fly by Wire, FMS-Flight Management System, GPS-Global Positioning
System, TCAS-Traffic Alert Collision Avoidance System and other systems.
